summary: The primary treatment approach involves discontinuation of excess vitamins, monitoring electrolyte balance, hydration support, possibly using hemodialysis if severe kidney issues arise; bisphosphonates such as zoledronic acid are effective against high blood calcium due to excessive vitamin intake while calcitonin may help temporarily reduce serum calcium concentrations though not recommended frequently because of rapid tolerance development. To directly answer your query about what agents treat vitamin D toxicity—discontinue offending medications first followed by supportive care measures tailored based upon severity indicators including lab values indicating elevated Ca++ concentration needing urgent intervention via dialytic means when necessary alongside pharmacological interventions targeting bone resorption inhibition through bisphophonate administration where indicated clinically. Chloroquine/Hydroxychloqouine/Ketoconozole do have roles elsewhere related indirectly yet aren't typically considered frontline therapy choices here despite being part-of-evidence reviewed during
